name: Mrs. f Double Chocolate Peanut Butter Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C).
2. In a double boiler or microwave, melt the semisweet chocolate over low heat until smooth.
3. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
4. In a small bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt.
5. In a medium bowl, whisk together sugars, butter, and peanut butter until well combined.
6. Add eggs and vanilla extract; mix until just combined.
7. Gradually add the flour mixture and milk chocolate chips to the dough, stirring until no streaks of flour remain visible.
8. Fold in the melted chocolate using a wooden spoon or spatula.
9. Drop rounded mounds of dough about 3 tablespoons onto an ungreased baking sheet, spacing them 2 inches apart.
10. Top each mound with one whole peanut.
11. Bake for 23 minutes or until set but still soft.
12. Let cool on the baking sheet for 30 seconds before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
